title: When Evaluating a Group: - Pause and Observe:
subtitle: See Beyond the Group Glow
description:
When evaluating a group:
- Pause and observe: "Am I judging individuals or the group as a whole?"
- Focus on specifics: Look at individual contributions or qualities instead of the overall impression.
- Compare individually: Evaluate members one by one to get a clearer picture.

Example: Judging team performance? Break it down into individual efforts, not just the group’s vibe.

howTo.title: Pause and Observe
howTo.summary: Before evaluating a group, take a moment to reflect. Ask yourself if you are judging individuals or the group as a whole. This helps to prevent bias.

howTo.title: Gather Individual Facts
howTo.summary: Collect three specific contributions or qualities from each group member. Record these with numeric measures to shift focus from general impressions to individual actions.

howTo.title: Compare Contributions
howTo.summary: Evaluate each member's contributions side by side. Look for differences in performance and document these to clarify individual roles and responsibilities.

howTo.title: Decide and Document Actions
howTo.summary: For each individual, write down one actionable item based on your evaluation. This ensures that feedback is specific and leads to measurable improvements.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track evaluations and actions over time. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/avoid-group-halo-bias-in-evaluations/.
